L1_headline: DAWN – Servier to acquire via $21.50 cash tender offer. | 03/06/26
L2_brief: Day One Biopharmaceuticals entered into a definitive merger agreement with Servier Pharmaceuticals LLC under which Servier will acquire all outstanding DAWN shares at $21.50 per share in cash via tender offer followed by a back-end merger under DGCL Section 251(h). The offer must commence within 15 business days and is subject to a majority tender condition, HSR clearance, and other customary conditions, with an outside date of December 6, 2026 (extendable by 150 days for regulatory approval). The Company termination fee is $87.7 million, and no financing condition applies.

L3_detailed.event: Day One Biopharmaceuticals signed an Agreement and Plan of Merger with Servier Pharmaceuticals LLC (Parent), Servier Detroit Inc. (Merger Sub), and Servier S.A.S. (Guarantor). Merger Sub will commence a cash tender offer for all outstanding DAWN shares, followed by a short-form merger under DGCL Section 251(h) without a stockholder vote.
key_figures: Offer price: $21.50 per share in cash
Offer must commence within 15 business days of March 6, 2026
Offer period: 20 business days from commencement
Outside date: December 6, 2026, extendable by 150 days for regulatory approval
Minimum tender condition: majority of outstanding shares
L3_detailed.deal_implications: The Board unanimously approved the transaction and recommends stockholders tender their shares. The merger will be effected under DGCL Section 251(h), requiring no stockholder vote post-tender. No financing condition exists; Servier S.A.S. guarantees all payment obligations. The tender offer has not yet commenced.
remaining_conditions: Majority of outstanding shares tendered in the Offer
Expiration or termination of HSR Act waiting period
Accuracy of Company representations and warranties (subject to MAE standard)
Company compliance with covenants in all material respects
Other conditions set forth in Exhibit A to the Merger Agreement
Commencement and completion of the tender offer
Filing of Schedule TO and Schedule 14D-9 with the SEC
risks_flagged: Competing offers or superior proposals could emerge; Company has fiduciary-out provisions for Superior Company Proposals
Potential stockholder litigation in connection with the Offer or Merger
Regulatory approval (HSR) not yet obtained; outside date extension of 150 days if regulatory approval is delayed
Customary non-solicitation restrictions with exceptions for unsolicited Superior Company Proposals
Risk that sufficient shares may not be tendered to satisfy the majority condition
